title: Publication or Submission of Quotations Without Specified Information
raw_text:
Publication or Submission of Quotations Without Specified Information

The Securities and Exchange Commission ("Commission") is proposing amendments to revise a rule that governs certain information gathering and review requirements that brokers and dealers must satisfy before initiating (or resuming) any quotation for a security in a quotation medium other than a national securities exchange. The proposed amendments would revise the rule to refer to only equity securities.

Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Option Exchanges - Change to the Penny Interval Program for April 1, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Option Exchanges - Change to the Penny Interval Program for April 1, 2026
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Pearl March 31, 2026 | 03:39 PM MIAX Option Exchanges - Change to the Penny Interval Program for April 1, 2026 In accordance with the MIAX Penny Interval Program annual review, MIAX will remove option classes in the Penny Interval Program that fall outside the 425 most actively traded multiply listed option classes on the first trading day of April. For a list of option classes that will be removed on April 1, 2026, please refer to the following Regulatory Circulars: MIAX Options RC 2026-47 MIAX Pearl Options RC 2026-47 MIAX Emerald Options RC 2026-36 MIAX Sapphire Options RC 2026-49 Please direct questions to the Regulatory Department at Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - April 1, 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - April 1, 2026 Fee Changes
Fee Change Alert MIAX Emerald March 31, 2026 | 03:18 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - April 1, 2026 Fee Changes On April 1, 2026, MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange, and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will amend the following fees pending filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission: MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange, and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange New Purge Liquidity Taker Report (“Purge Report”) will be available for subscription for each respective exchange beginning April 1, 2026 as previously announced in this Alert The subscription fees for each respective exchange are $30,000/annually or $4,000/monthly The annual subscriptions for each of the existing Liquidity Taker Event Reports will be amended, per exchange, per report as follows: Simple Liquidity Taker Report (“Simple Order Report”) – from $24,000 to $30,000 Complex Liquidity Taker Report (“Complex Order Report”) – from $24,000 to $30,000 MIAX Pearl Options Exchange does not offer the Complex Order Liquidity Taker Report Resting Simple Liquidity Taker Report (“Resting Simple Order Report”) – from $12,000 to $15,000 The new rates above will apply to new subscriptions initiated on or after April 1, 2026 (current annual subscriptions will continue through their existing expiration date) Policies related to all Liquidity Taker Event Reports: Discount Program – Members who purchase a Simple, Complex, Resting Simple or Purge Liquidity Taker Report may purchase additional reports on the same exchange at a 33.3% discount Monthly subscriptions for all reports will no longer be available after December 31, 2026 Beginning January 1, 2027, annual subscriptions will be offered on a semi-annual cycle (January 1 and July 1) and any annual subscription that is off-cycle will be prorated MIAX Sapphire Floor Transactions New surcharge and Floor Broker Enhanced Complex Rebate The Exchange will assess a per contract surcharge of $0.20 to Floor Market Makers when they are a counterparty to an executed Priority Customer Complex Qualified Floor Order (“cQFO”) in all classes For such trades, the Exchange will provide a per contract Floor Broker Enhanced Complex Rebate of ($0.20) to the Floor Broker when a Floor Market Maker is the counterparty to an executed Priority Customer cQFO in all classes (this Floor Broker Enhanced Complex Rebate is in addition to the Floor Broker Breakup Credit) The surcharge and Floor Broker Enhanced Complex Rebate will not apply to cQCC, cC2C, strategy-related, or other non-complex transactions Attached are highlighted summaries of the fee changes. Complete details will be contained in the April 2026 exchange fee schedules, when posted on the MIAX website at MIAX Options Fee Schedule , MIAX Pearl Options Fee Schedule , MIAX Emerald Options Fee Schedule , MIAX Sapphire Fee Schedule . For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177. For assistance, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. MIAX Options Highlight of Fee Changes 04012026 MIAX Pearl Options Highlighted Fee Changes 04012026 MIAX Emerald Highlighted Fee Changes 04012026 MIAX Sapphire Highlight of Fee Changes 04012026

title: MIAX Sapphire Options - Changes to Billing System Rules to Allow Members to Provide Alternative Payment Instructions for Certain Fees Starting April 1, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options - Changes to Billing System Rules to Allow Members to Provide Alternative Payment Instructions for Certain Fees Starting April 1, 2026
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire March 31, 2026 | 11:11 AM MIAX Sapphire Options - Changes to Billing System Rules to Allow Members to Provide Alternative Payment Instructions for Certain Fees Starting April 1, 2026 MIAX Sapphire (the “Exchange”) Rule 208 allows, upon request, the Exchange to waive the requirement for a Member to designate a Clearing Member for debiting fees. If waived, the Member must provide, as agreed to by the Exchange, alternative payment instructions for the Exchange to debit certain fees. Starting April 1, 2026, alternative payment instructions may only be used for certain fee categories determined by the Exchange. For more information on the billing system changes, please refer to the following Regulatory Circular: MIAX Sapphire Options RC 2026-48 For questions regarding the alternative payment instruction, please contact the Sales Department at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177. Direct Regulatory questions to the Regulatory Department at Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Emerald March 31, 2026 | 10:27 AM MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - Updated FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements The MIAX Emerald Exchange interface specifications have been updated to support the following enhancements. FOI Interface Specification v1.3 : Market Maker on PRIME/cPRIME Contra Functionality This optional feature allows members to select Market Maker (MM) on the contra side of PRIME and cPRIME. Updates to the FOI Interface Specification are as follows: Market Maker on PRIME/cPRIME Contra Functionality Enhanced New Order Cross message to select Market Maker (MM) as a valid “CustomerOrFirm” value. Enhanced New Order Cross – Multileg message to select Market Maker (MM) as a valid “CustomerOrFirm” value. Additional Details : These new features are pending filing with the SEC by MIAX Emerald and an activation schedule will be announced in a future alert. These changes will be available in the MIAX Emerald Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment in May. Visit MIAX Emerald Interface Specifications for additional details. Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to discuss any specific issues, update configurations and to arrange interface testing.

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Updated MEI, AIS and FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Updated MEI, AIS and FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Options March 30, 2026 | 10:02 AM MIAX Options Exchange - Updated MEI, AIS and FOI Interface Specifications to Support Upcoming Enhancements The MIAX Options Exchange interface specifications have been updated to support the following enhancements. MEI Interface Specification v2.11, AIS Interface Specification v2.6, and FOI Interface Specification v2.7 : Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Functionality This optional feature enhances the Mass Cancellation functionality by providing members the ability to remove a subset of its quotes in the system atomically. With the introduction of a new field “SLAP Codes” in the Simple Bulk Quote Message, members can specify custom SLAP codes on some or all of its liquidity, providing more granular control of the Mass Liquidity Cancel Request when attempting to “Selectively Purge” specific targeted liquidity. Aggregate Risk Manager (ARM) Origin Multiplier Functionality This optional feature allows members to configure “ARM Origin Multiplier” for each contra-side origin type. The multiplier will be applied to each ARM-eligible trade size based on the contra-side origin type, allowing members to set up an ARM profile for which trades against certain origin types are weighted differently than others in the ARM Trade Percentage Calculation. Note that for firms using old application protocols, their current settings will extend to all contra-types at 100% weightings by default. Quote Width Relief Notification Functionality This optional feature allows members to subscribe to the “Free Trading Quote Width Relief Notification” to receive a real-time notification in the event MIAX grants relief to the valid quote width parameters during trading hours. Note that this relief is only applicable for the current trading day. Mass Cancel Trigger Reason on Line Disconnect This optional feature allows members to receive a mass cancel trigger reason specific to Line Disconnect. Liquidity Origin Information Functionality This optional feature allows members to get the Origin of the liquidity that initiated a Liquidity Seeking Event (LSE). Note a blank Origin will be sent in the event an LSE is initiated by multiple liquidities with different origin types. Market Maker on PRIME/cPRIME Contra Functionality This optional feature allows members to have a Market Maker (MM) on the contra side of PRIME and cPRIME. Updates to the MEI Interface Specification are as follows: SLAP Functionality New field “SLAP Codes” in the Simple Bulk Quote Message and Mass Liquidity Cancel Request New field “SLAP Codes” and new Mass Cancel Scope in the Mass Liquidity Cancel Request Enhanced Liquidity Protection Reset Request message that includes new field “SLAP Codes”. New SLAP Protection Trigger Notification New cancel reason due to SLAP in Cancel Notification New status codes subject to SLAP in the following response messages: Simple Bulk Quote Response Mass Liquidity Cancel Response Liquidity Protection Reset Response ARM Origin Multiplier Functionality ARM Origin Multiplier related fields added, and “Message Type” change to RS in Simple ARM Settings Update Request ARM Origin Multiplier related fields added in ARM Simple Settings Update Response ARM Origin Multiplier related fields added, and “Message Type” change to RN in Simple ARM Protection Settings Notification Quote Width Relief Notification Functionality Introduction of New Free Trading Quote Width Relief Notification Quote Width Relief Notification renamed as Opening Quote Width Relief Notification Mass Cancel Trigger Reason on Line Disconnect New “Trigger Reason” value to indicate Line Disconnect in Simple Quote Protection Trigger Notification message. New “Trigger Reason” value to indicate Line Disconnect in Complex Liquidity Protection Trigger Notification message. Updates to the AIS Interface Specification are as follows: Liquidity Origin Information Functionality Enhanced Simple Liquidity Seeking Event Notification that includes a new field “Origin” to indicate the origin of the liquidity initiating the Liquidity Seeking Event. Enhanced Complex Liquidity Seeking Event Notification that includes a new field “Origin” to indicate the origin of the liquidity initiating the Liquidity Seeking Event. Updates to the FOI Interface Specification are as follows: Market Maker on PRIME/cPRIME Contra Functionality Enhanced New Order Cross message to have Market Maker (MM) as a valid “CustomerOrFirm” value. Enhanced New Order Cross – Multileg message to have Market Maker (MM) as a valid “CustomerOrFirm” value. Additional Details : These new features are pending SEC approval and an activation schedule for each will be announced in a future alert. These changes will be available in the MIAX Options Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment in June. Visit MIAX Options Interface Specifications for additional details. Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to discuss any specific issues, update configurations and to arrange interface testing.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald March 26, 2026 | 02:30 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026 Beginning April 1, 2026, the new Purge Liquidity Taker Report will be available for the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options exchanges. Purge Liquidity Taker Report Details : The daily Purge Liquidity Taker Report will provide recipients with liquidity response/taker time details for executions against the recipient firm’s simple order book quotes, when the quoting firm sends a purge message either before or after the executions occurred. The report includes liquidity taker and purge message timestamps related to: All trades against a resting quote that occurred within 100 microseconds before receipt of the purge message All trades against a resting quote that occurred within 200 microseconds after the receipt of the purge message. Liquidity taker and purge message timestamps are at the outer edge of the exchange network. The reports will be available via the MIAX SFTP site. The Purge Liquidity Taker Report will be subject to fees announced in a future alert and pending filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The full content, timeframes and data scope of the Purge Liquidity Taker Report can be found in Rule 531 for each exchange. Further details regarding reports can be found at: MIAX Options Reports MIAX Pearl Options Reports MIAX Emerald Options Reports MIAX Sapphire Options Reports Members can request reports and specifications by contacting Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire March 26, 2026 | 02:30 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Purge Liquidity Taker Report Launching April 1, 2026 Beginning April 1, 2026, the new Purge Liquidity Taker Report will be available for the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options exchanges. Purge Liquidity Taker Report Details : The daily Purge Liquidity Taker Report will provide recipients with liquidity response/taker time details for executions against the recipient firm’s simple order book quotes, when the quoting firm sends a purge message either before or after the executions occurred. The report includes liquidity taker and purge message timestamps related to: All trades against a resting quote that occurred within 100 microseconds before receipt of the purge message All trades against a resting quote that occurred within 200 microseconds after the receipt of the purge message. Liquidity taker and purge message timestamps are at the outer edge of the exchange network. The reports will be available via the MIAX SFTP site. The Purge Liquidity Taker Report will be subject to fees announced in a future alert and pending filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The full content, timeframes and data scope of the Purge Liquidity Taker Report can be found in Rule 531 for each exchange. Further details regarding reports can be found at: MIAX Options Reports MIAX Pearl Options Reports MIAX Emerald Options Reports MIAX Sapphire Options Reports Members can request reports and specifications by contacting Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177.

title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and April 11, 2026 Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and April 11, 2026 Testing
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Sapphire March 24, 2026 | 03:00 PM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and April 11, 2026 Testing The MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange will upgrade the operating system for all production MIAX Sapphire Options extranet edge network switches in the NY4 data center after market close on Friday, April 10, 2026. It is recommended that all firms with MIAX Sapphire Options 10 Gig ULL Cross Connects perform access tests and subscribe to market data through the application layer on Saturday, April 11, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 10:00 a.m. EDT. Please note that the upgrade will only be performed on the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange edge network operating system switches and will not impact other MIAX exchanges. Once your firm's testing is completed, please inform MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and March 28, 2026 Testing
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and March 28, 2026 Testing
Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Options March 16, 2026 | 04:02 PM MIAX Options Exchange - Notice of Network Switch Operating System Upgrade and March 28, 2026 Testing The MIAX Options Exchange will upgrade the operating system for all production MIAX Options extranet edge network switches in NY4 data center on the evening of Friday, March 27, 2026. It is recommended that all firms with MIAX Options 10 Gig ULL Cross Connects perform access tests and subscribe to market data through the application layer on Saturday, March 28, 2026 between 8:30 a.m. and 10:00 a.m. EDT. Please note that the upgrade will only be performed on MIAX Options Exchange edge network operating system switches and will not impact other MIAX Exchanges. Once your firm's testing is completed, please inform MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Option Exchanges - Application Message Rate Guidance for eQuotes, IOC and ISO Orders
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Trading Alert, Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Option Exchanges - Application Message Rate Guidance for eQuotes, IOC and ISO Orders
Trading Alert, Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Options March 12, 2026 | 12:55 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Option Exchanges - Application Message Rate Guidance for eQuotes, IOC and ISO Orders Effective April 1, 2026, in the interest of protecting the performance profile of the MIAX trading systems and to provide actionable guidelines regarding acceptable application-level messaging activity, members are prohibited from exceeding prescribed message rates for MEI IOC and ISO eQuotes for MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options, and for MEO IOC and ISO orders for MIAX Pearl Options and MIAX Sapphire Options, when processed by the exchange applications. For additional information on Application Message Rate Guidance, please refer to the following Regulatory Circulars: MIAX Options RC 2026-34 MIAX Pearl Options RC 2026-34 MIAX Emerald Options RC 2026-28 MIAX Sapphire Options RC 2026-35 Additionally, MIAX now offers a complimentary Message Rate Report to assist in monitoring Firm message rates. Please contact TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com to setup daily delivery of the report. For questions regarding this guidance, including the related measurement and / or monitoring methodologies, please contact the Regulatory Department at Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

Technical Alert, Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire March 06, 2026 | 03:02 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options, Equities and Futures Markets - Daylight Saving Time - March 2026 Please be advised the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, MIAX Sapphire Options, MIAX Pearl Equities and MIAX Futures Exchanges are scheduled to begin Daylight Saving Time at 2:00 a.m. ET on Sunday, March 8, 2026. title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol CULP Effective Friday, March 6, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol CULP Effective Friday, March 6, 2026
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Sapphire March 06, 2026 | 07:30 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Change to the Market for Underlying Security Used for Openings on MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options for Symbol CULP Effective Friday, March 6, 2026 Culp, Inc. (“CULP”) transferred primary listing from the New York Stock Exchange to the NASDAQ Capital Market (“NASDAQ”) effective today, Friday, March 6, 2026. MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options will use NASDAQ as the “market for the underlying security” for the purpose of Openings on the Exchange. For more information, please refer to the following Regulatory Circulars: MIAX Options Regulatory Circular 2026-30 MIAX Pearl Options Regulatory Circular 2026-30 MIAX Emerald Options Regulatory Circular 2026-25 MIAX Sapphire Options Regulatory Circular 2026-31 Please direct questions to the Regulatory Department at Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade date March 3, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade date March 3, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Emerald March 03, 2026 | 07:53 AM MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options - Effective for trade date March 3, 2026 2X OPENING and INTRADAY Valid and Priority Quote Spread Relief in All Symbols Multiplier: 2X Reason: In maintenance of a fair and orderly market. Time: OPENING and INTRADAY Subject Summary: Please be advised, effective for trade date March 3, 2026, the MIAX Regulatory Department has granted 2 times OPENING and INTRADAY quote parameter relief for all symbols on MIAX Options and MIAX Emerald Options. Please note, standard quote width is $5 wide, two (2) times width is $10. The quote width listed in the following will be two (2) times the listed width. https://www.miaxglobal.com/markets/us-options/miax-options/market-maker-requirements https://www.miaxglobal.com/markets/us-options/emerald-options/market-maker-requirements For questions or comments, please contact the Regulatory Department at regulatory@miaxglobal.com.

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Pearl Equities March 05, 2026 | 02:41 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - 2026 Market Wide Circuit Breaker Testing on Saturday, April 11, 2026 As previously announced in the January 13, 2026 Alert , MIAX Pearl Equities, in coordination with other market centers and the Securities Information Processors (“SIPs”), will participate in the 2026 annual Market Wide Circuit Breaker (“MWCB”) tests. In accordance with MIAX Pearl Equities Rule 2622, Limit Up-Limit Down Plan and Trading Halts, certain MIAX Pearl Equities Members have mandatory participation requirements in the annual MWCB tests. MIAX Pearl Equities will notify Members that are required to participate in the annual MWCB tests prior to the first annual test date. Members must register with Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com prior to testing in order to participate in MWCB tests and are required to submit attestations to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com . Please note, these tests are systematically monitored for exceptions and the results are reviewed on the following Monday, not in real time. Successful testing includes: Receive and process MWCB halt messages from the SIPs; Receive and process resume messages from the SIPs following a MWCB halt; Receive and process market data from the SIPs relevant to MWCB halts; and Send orders following a Level 1 or Level 2 MWCB halt in a manner consistent with usual trading behavior Please refer to MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange Regulatory Circular 2026-02 for more information. Test Dates Saturday, April 11, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #1 Saturday, July 25, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #2 Saturday, September 12, 2026: Dedicated MWCB Level 1, 2 & 3 Saturday, December 5, 2026: Failover / MWCB Level 1 & 2 Test #3 For questions regarding the MWCB test, please contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Updated DoM Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Launch of Financial Futures
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Futures March 02, 2026 | 12:39 PM MIAX Futures Exchange - Onyx Trading Platform - Updated DoM Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Launch of Financial Futures As previously announced in the February 3, 2026 Alert , MIAX Futures will be introducing functionality to support trading of financial futures products on the MIAX Futures Onyx trading platform. These features are expected to be rolled out to users on Sunday, May 17, 2026 for the Monday, May 18, 2026 trade date. The MIAX Futures Financials Market Identifier Code (MIC) is XMFE. Financial futures products will trade in the NY5 data center. Updated Depth of Market Feed Interface Specification to indicate that Settlement Price Update messages will now also be sent for Complex Instruments. Please see section 4.6 of the updated spec available here : Depth of Market (DoM) Feed - Version 1.1b . Financial Products Launch Schedule: B100 Tini Future - Sunday, May 17, 2026 for the Monday, May 18, 2026 trade date B500 Tini Future - Sunday, May 31, 2026 for the Monday, June 1, 2026 trade date B500 Standard Future - Sunday, June 7, 2026 for the Monday, June 8, 2026 trade date Important Dates : Industry Testing is scheduled for the following dates: Saturday, April 25, 2026 Saturday, May 9, 2026 Saturday, May 16, 2026 Support for financial futures trading is scheduled for Sunday, May 17, 2026 for the Monday, May 18, 2026 trade date Firm Test Bed and Production Cross-Connect connectivity requests are now being accepted The Firm Test Bed is now available for all features Updated Specifications are now available Market data agreements are now available Production interface access (FOI and FEI) and market data (TOM and DOM) will be available beginning Monday, April 13, 2026 Market participants should ensure clearing arrangements are in place with Options Clearing Corporation (OCC) members as MIAX Futures financial futures products will clear through OCC MIAX Futures Onyx Financial Futures Resources : Please visit the MIAX Futures Onyx site for the most recent information Updated MIAX Futures Onyx Interface Specifications are now available MIAX Futures Onyx Exchange Fees Additional information on support for financial futures on MIAX Futures Onyx will be provided in future alerts. Final product specifications remain subject to the submission of certain rule filings to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. To learn more about MIAX Futures Onyx or new financial futures products please contact SalesFutures@miaxglobal.com . title: NYSE Options: 2026 Penny Interval Program Rebalance (Final Reminder), GTC Order Handling
raw_text:
NYSE Options: 2026 Penny Interval Program Rebalance (Final Reminder), GTC Order Handling
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On April 1, 2026 , NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options will complete the 2026 annual rebalance of the Penny Interval Program for Options ("Program") by removing issues that met the criteria for removal, as previously announced . The list of issues scheduled for removal, along with current Program issues, is available here . Good-'Til-Cancelled (GTC) Order Handling GTC orders in increments that will no longer be valid will be canceled after the close of market today, March 31, 2026. OUT messages will be issued for canceled orders prior to Done-For-Day (DFD) messages. Complex GTC orders will not be canceled and will remain in force. Contact Information For questions regarding the Penny Interval Program for Options, contact the NYSE Trading Operations Desk at 212-896-2830, option 1. For all other questions regarding these changes, contact rmteam@nyse.com .

title: NYSE American - New Security Notification for Symbol NINE
raw_text:
NYSE American - New Security Notification for Symbol NINE
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES; Services: Trading
NYSE American has determined that the Common Stock issued by Nine Energy Service, Inc. is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker NINE with CUSIP 65441V200 on March 31, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security NINE until its second day of trading on NYSE American. title: NYSE Group: Market Wide Circuit Breaker Test April 11, 2026
raw_text:
NYSE Group: Market Wide Circuit Breaker Test April 11, 2026
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
On Saturday, April 11, 2026, NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE National and NYSE Texas will participate in the Securities Information Processors' (SIPs) industry testing of Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Level 1 and 2. NYSE Group equities markets will be available from their primary data sites in Mahwah from 9:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. ET. Designated required participants for the October 2026 Reg. SCI test on NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE National and NYSE Texas are required to participate in at least one MWCB test, per year. Firms will be notified of their requirement in April 2026. The script for the test can be found here . The testing requirements below must be completed for each NYSE Group equities exchange for which a firm has been designated as a required tester: Receive and process MWCB halt messages from the SIPs. Receive and process resume messages from the SIPs following a MWCB halt. Receive and process market data from the SIPs relevant to the MWCB halt. Send orders following a Level 1 or Level 2 MWCB halt in a manner consistent with usual trading behavior. Firms should register to test at trading@nyse.com to have their participation recorded. Following the test, firms must return the participation checklist to Regscitesting@nyse.com . Remaining 2026 SIP Failover/MWCB Saturday Testing Dates April 11, 2026 - SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #1 July 25, 2026 - SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #2 September 12, 2026 - MWCB Level 1/2/3 Test December 5, 2026 - SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #3 Please contact your NYSE Relationship Manager or rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this test.

title: NYSE Arca Equity Membership On-Ramp Program
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Equity Membership On-Ramp Program
Markets: NYSE ARCA EQUITIES; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
NYSE Arca has introduced a program for new Equity Trading Permit (ETP) Holders designed to reduce the cost of entry by providing tiered discounts on trading license fees, connectivity port fees, and select market data products (the "Program"). The Program offers graduated fee relief over an 18-month period, enabling new participants to scale into the NYSE Arca equities marketplace at a reduced cost Eligibility To qualify for the Program, a firm must not have been approved as an NYSE Arca ETP Holder within the prior 18 months. Each firm may enroll in the Program only once. Affiliates of existing ETP Holders (defined as any ETP Holder under 75% common ownership or control) are not eligible to participate. Discount Schedule Discounting begins in the calendar month in which the firm's new ETP membership application is approved. Eligible firms for the Program may receive the following tiered discounts: Category Months 1-6 Discount Months 7-12 Discount Months 13-18 Discount Trading Permit Fees 100% 50% 25% Order Entry/Quote Entry Ports: Fees for the first 10 ports 100% 50% 25% Drop Copy Ports: Fees for the first 10 ports 100% 50% 25% Market Data Fees: · NYSE Arca Integrated Feed · NYSE Arca Book · NYSE Arca Aggregated Lite · NYSE Arca BBO · NYSE Arca Trades · NYSE Arca Order Imbalances 100% 50% 25% The Market Data fees that are eligible for the Program are: Access Fees (general and Per User, if applicable), Professional User Fees (internal use only), Non-Professional User fee (for external use, including Enterprise Fees), Non-Display Fees, Redistribution Fee, and Multiple Data Feed Fee ("Eligible Market Data Fees"). The Program's discounts are not available to a ETP Holder subject to the Digital Media Enterprise Fee or Professional User Fees for any data externally distributed to professional subscribers. A firm that was a subscriber to any of the Eligible Market Data Fees within the prior 18 months before becoming approved as a new ETP holder is ineligible for Program's Market Data fee discounts. Program discounts cannot be combined with any other discounts applicable to Eligible Market Data Fees. Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with questions about this update.

title: Regulatory Transaction Fee Rate Adjustment per SEC Section 31
raw_text:
Regulatory Transaction Fee Rate Adjustment per SEC Section 31
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
In connection with the SEC's Fee Rate Advisory for Fiscal Year 2026 , the Securities and Exchange Commission has announced that the Section 31 transaction fee rate applicable to securities transactions on national securities exchanges and over-the-counter markets will be adjusted to $20.60 per million dollars, effective April 4, 2026. Until that date, the current rate of $0.00 per million dollars will remain in effect. NYSE, NYSE Arca, NYSE American, NYSE Chicago, NYSE National, NYSE Arca Options, and NYSE American Options will adjust the Regulatory Transaction Fee to reflect the new rate of $20.60 per million dollars for all trades settling on or after April 4, 2026. This adjustment applies to all sell transactions executed on or after the trade date of April 2, 2026. For additional details regarding the SEC's Fee Rate Advisory, please refer to the SEC's announcement at sec.gov .

title: NYSE Group - NYSE Pillar and Trade Ops Portal (TOP) Improvements
raw_text:
NYSE Group - NYSE Pillar and Trade Ops Portal (TOP) Improvements
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning March 23, 2026, NYSE Group equities and options exchanges will implement performance improvements and introduce new functionality on NYSE Pillar and the NYSE Pillar Trade Ops Portal (TOP), including: Repricing optimizations Market data publication improvements Max Notional Check for Complex Options All market data sessions available in TOP GUI and REST API Updates to outbound timestamp, as previously announced Changes on NYSE Pillar are available for testing across NYSE Group equities and options certification environments. TOP functionality will be available for testing on April 20, 2026. Repricing Optimizations (NYSE Equities and Options Markets) Repricing logic will be optimized; functionality for repricing orders and quotes will not change. Improvements to Market Data Publication (NYSE Equities Markets Only) Market data updates will be improved by publishing executions and related updates incrementally as orders interact at each price level. Max Notional Check for Complex Options (NYSE Options Markets Only) The single-order notional check will be modified to use the Series Contract Multiplier as part of the calculation for Complex Orders. This change was previously applied to Single-Leg Orders in Q3 2025. Market Data Sessions Available in TOP GUI and REST API (NYSE Equities and Options Markets) All market data sessions will be available in the TOP GUI and REST API beginning on Monday, April 27, 2026. NYSE Pillar Implementation Timeline Market Production Date NYSE National Monday, March 23, 2026 NYSE American Monday, March 30, 2026 NYSE Arca Thursday, April 2, 2026 NYSE Monday, April 13, 2026 NYSE Texas Thursday, April 16, 2026 NYSE American Options Monday, April 27, 2026 NYSE Arca Options Thursday, April 30, 2026 Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about these updates.

title: NYSE: Reminder - Direct Debit via DTCC and Invoice Changes Effective April 2026
raw_text:
NYSE: Reminder - Direct Debit via DTCC and Invoice Changes Effective April 2026
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning in April 2026, NYSE will transition to Direct Debit via DTCC ("Direct Debit") as the default payment method for certain fees and introduce a new invoice format that summarizes execution fees, as previously announced . DTCC Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees The transition to Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees will be implemented in April 2026. Firms may opt out of Direct Debit by completing the Direct Debit Opt-Out Request form and returning it to CRS at crs@nyse.com by March 24, 2026 . Invoice Format Update Beginning with the April 2026 bill, a new invoice format will be introduced that summarizes fees by the liquidity tag returned on executions. A full list of liquidity tags may be found on the NYSE Pillar Reason Codes and Liquidity Indicators . For questions about these changes, please contact rmteam@nyse.com .

raw_text:

Effective March 16, 2026, options on the MSCI EAFE Index (MXEA) and the MSCI Emerging Markets Index (MXEF) will be listed and available for trading on NYSE American Options. title: Testing Opportunity: OPRA Capacity Test Reminder - April 11, 2026
raw_text:
Testing Opportunity: OPRA Capacity Test Reminder - April 11, 2026
1 March 25, 2026 To: OPRA Multicast Data Subscribers Subject: Testing Opportunity: OPRA Capacity Test Reminder - April 11, 2026 OPRA traffic projections are based on messages per 100-millisecond (MPHM) intervals. The use of a 10- millisecond interval reflects system utilization during bursts of traffic. The bandwidth required to receive OPRA data is reflected in Gigabits. Please note that the traffic projections are for one stream only. For fault tolerance purposes, two redundant streams of data are available from SIAC. For those Multicast Data Subscribers who elect to receive both streams of data, the bandwidth requirements would be double. SIAC provides Saturday capacity testing opportunities three times a year to allow Multicast Data Subscribers to validate processing OPRA projected maximum output traffic rates. All OPRA Multicast Data Subscribers are invited to participate in capacity testing occurring on the same dates as the SIP Failover tests as follows: 2026 OPRA Capacity Testing Schedule • Sat 4/11/26: from approximately 12:00pm - 1:00pm ET • Sat 7/25/26: from approximately 12:00pm - 1:00pm ET • Sat 12/5/26: from approximately 12:00pm - 1:00pm ET The maximum output traffic rates for OPRA data services will be as follows: OPRA Capacity Projections Effective Date 100-Milliseconds 10-Milliseconds Total Messages Per Day (billions) Maximum Output Rate per Output Line MPHM (Thousands Msg / 100ms) Maximum Output Traffic Rates (Million Msg /100ms) Bandwidth (Gigabits /100ms) Peak Packets (Thousand packets / 100ms) Maximum Output Traffic Rates (Millions Msg/10ms) Bandwidth (Gigabits /10ms) Peak Packets (Thousand packets / 10ms) 7/2026 16.134 4.521 887 1.824 0.511 101 287 625 1/2027 17.011 4.762 929 1.923 0.539 107 300 625 7/2027 17.923 5.019 979 2.026 0.566 109 315 625 1/2028 18.905 5.298 1043 2.137 0.597 115 330 625 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 2 Retransmissions The required bandwidth should be increased by 10% to account for retransmissions. Latency The median latency for OPRA is under 19 microseconds. Message latency is measured beginning with the time-stamp taken as an inbound Participant message arrives at the network entrance to the OPRA environment, through processing by the system into a consolidated message for Multicast Data Subscribers, to the time-stamp taken as the outbound message arrives at the network exit from the environment. These time-stamps are taken and correlated by a process external to the data processing applications. If the external process cannot correlate an inbound message to its corresponding outbound message or measures negative latency for a message, the message is excluded from broader latency calculations such as median message latency. Test Registration Each OPRA Data Subscriber participating in the test should register at CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com . Data Subscribers who receive OPRA from connectivity service providers other than ICE Global Network (IGN) or the NMS Network must contact their connectivity service providers to coordinate testing. *Please note that if no Multicast Data Subscribers register for capacity testing, capacity testing will not take place. Questions Questions regarding the bandwidth requirements should be addressed to: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com. Multicast Data Subscribers can also contact the SIAC NMS Product Management Desk at 212-656-8177, Option 2, Mon - Fri, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, ET. If support team members are engaged with other customers, please leave a detailed voice message of the purpose of your call, which will produce an email of your message to the support team.

title: Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, April 11, 2026, Reminder
raw_text:
Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, April 11, 2026, Reminder
1 March 18, 2026 TO: OPRA and OPRA Global Trading Hours (GTH) Multicast Data Subscribers SUBJECT: Testing Opportunity - OPRA and GTH Failover to Secondary Data Center Testing: Saturday, April 11, 2026, Reminder Failover Testing As previously announced, SIAC will conduct OPRA and GTH failover testing to the secondary data center, as well as Market-Wide Circuit Breaker (MWCB) Breach Level 1 and 2 (7% breach and 13% breach) testing with the OPRA exchanges on Saturday, April 11, 2026, from 8:30 am - 11:00 am ET. In addition, OPRA GTH will also conduct failover testing to the secondary data center, from 7:30 am - 9:15 am ET. All OPRA and GTH Multicast Data Subscribers are invited to participate in these tests to validate expected processing within your systems. Remaining 2026 SIP Failover/MWCB Saturday Testing Schedule • Sat 7/25/26: SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #2 • Sat 9/12/26: MWCB Level 1/2/3 Test • Sat 12/5/26: SIP Failover / MWCB Level 1/2 Test #3 Failover Test Details The GTH failover to the secondary data center can occur at any time during the 7:55 am - 8:15am ET time window, with the OPRA failover occurring at any time during the 10:00 am - 11:00 am ET time window, to simulate an unexpected real-time event requiring a failover. Participating exchanges will continue to generate test data, which will be disseminated over the production multicast lines sourced from the secondary data center. title: OPRA System Hardware Upgrade - Activation Date: Monday, September 21, 2026
raw_text:
OPRA System Hardware Upgrade - Activation Date: Monday, September 21, 2026
March 4, 2026 TO: OPRA Multicast Data Subscribers SUBJECT: OPRA System Hardware Upgrade - Activation Date: Monday, September 21, 2026 What You Should Know SIAC will be upgrading the OPRA Pillar platform to next generation hardware. The targeted activation date for this upgrade will be Monday, September 21, 2026. What is Changing? • Although this upgrade does not impact the multicast feed, the OPRA SIP will be offering industry test opportunities for this change. Testing Opportunities and Activation • Industry Test #1: Saturday, August 8, 2026 • Industry Test #2: Saturday, August 22, 2026 • Confidence Test: Saturday, September 19, 2026 • Activation: Monday, September 21, 2026 Test Registration • Each OPRA Data Subscriber participating in the test should register at CTA-OPRA- Support@siac.com. Data Subscribers who receive OPRA from connectivity service providers other than ICE Global Network (IGN) or the NMS Network must contact their connectivity service providers to coordinate testing. Technical Inquiries • NMS Product Management Support Email: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com • NMS Product Management Support Line: 212.656.8177, Option 2 (Monday through Friday, 9:00AM - 5:00PM ET) Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005
